rationale: Option A's load-bearing assumption — that PostToolUse tool_response carries a child-session identifier for Agent/Task — has zero checked-in evidence in the repo. Repository-wide grep for tool_response.agent_id/tool_response.session_id returns zero matches; no test fixture, no sample payload, no documented surface. Option B uses the only Claude-Code-documented surface (hooks-reference.md:1059-1112) where SubagentStart/SubagentStop payloads carry agent_id, agent_type, and agent_transcript_path directly. The 3-layer wiring (hooks.json binding → bash adapter → Python handler registered in _dispatch_table) has 4+ clean precedents. Schema column rename child_session_id → agent_id is mechanical (already flagged at issue line 181).
rule: Bind SubagentStart/SubagentStop lifecycle hooks for subagent_runs producer
alternatives_rejected: Option A: extract child_session_id from tool_response inside post_tool_use.py — load-bearing assumption unverified by any checked-in evidence; implementation would be either dead code or brittle inference; ENH-2497 dependency unlanded adds coordination risk
